name: Sri Lanka
intro: Sri Lanka's professionals have long exported their skills — to the Gulf, to Singapore, to remote roles with Western firms — and international certification is the passport that travel requires. At home, Colombo's IT export sector keeps growing through global delivery centres, the port city development adds construction programmes, and apparel firms run compliance-driven change. PMP and Scrum credentials are respected across all of it, and they anchor CVs aimed abroad. Our live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, effectively local time for Sri Lanka, making the two-day workshop seamless from Colombo or Kandy. English instruction, an international cohort and sponsorship-ready invoices complete the package. After class we support your exam application, provide realistic mocks, and keep the trainer reachable for questions until you pass.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Sri Lanka?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Sri Lanka?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Sri Lanka.
